perturbation.original_text: Section 2.01 Equipment Orders. Upon the written acknowledgment by the Company and Lessor of each jointly approved purchase agreement, purchase order or invoice ("Purchase Order"), the Company, pursuant to the agency granted to it by Lessor in Article I hereof, may order, receive, accept the Equipment to be leased in accordance with the Economic Terms (as hereafter defined and described). Upon and as of the date of acceptance of the Equipment by the Company and satisfaction of the conditions precedent provided for in the Lease: (a) Lessor shall be unconditionally obligated to purchase such Equipment pursuant to the terms of the applicable Purchase Order and to lease such Equipment to the Company pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Lease and the applicable completed Schedule; and (b) the Company shall be unconditionally obligated to lease such Equipment from Lessor pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Lease and the applicable completed Schedule.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 2.01 Equipment Orders. Upon the written acknowledgment by the Company and Lessor of each jointly approved purchase agreement, purchase order or invoice ("Purchase Order"), the Company, pursuant to the agency granted to it by Lessor in Article I hereof, may order, receive, accept the Equipment to be leased in accordance with the Economic Terms (as hereafter defined and described). Upon and as of the date of acceptance of the Equipment by the Company and satisfaction of the conditions precedent provided for in the Lease: (a) Lessor shall be conditionally obligated to purchase such Equipment pursuant to the terms of the applicable Purchase Order and to lease such Equipment to the Company pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Lease and the applicable completed Schedule; and (b) the Company shall be conditionally obligated to lease such Equipment from Lessor pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Lease and the applicable completed Schedule.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that 'Lessor shall be unconditionally obligated to purchase such Equipment'. The modification changes 'unconditionally' to 'conditionally', creating a contradiction. It is now uncertain whether the Lessor is truly obligated, impacting Section 2.01 overall.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2.01 Equipment Orders. Upon the written acknowledgment by the Company and Lessor of each jointly approved purchase agreement, purchase order or invoice ("Purchase Order"), the Company, pursuant to the agency granted to it by Lessor in Article I hereof, may order, receive, accept the Equipment to be leased in accordance with the Economic Terms (as hereafter defined and described). Upon and as of the date of acceptance of the Equipment by the Company and satisfaction of the conditions precedent provided for in the Lease: (a) Lessor shall be conditionally obligated to purchase such Equipment pursuant to the terms of the applicable Purchase Order and to lease such Equipment to the Company pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Lease and the applicable completed Schedule; and (b) the Company shall be conditionally obligated to lease such Equipment from Lessor pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Lease and the applicable completed Schedule.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Lessor 'shall be unconditionally obligated', while the changed text modifies this to 'shall be conditionally obligated'. This directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement, creating uncertainty about the Lessor's commitment. Because the change of 'unconditionally obligated' to 'conditionally obligated' introduces conditionality where there was none before, this represents a strong in-text contradiction according to the structural flaws definition.

perturbation.original_text: Section 3.01 Termination. (a) So long as no default exists and is continuing hereunder or under the Lease, either party may terminate this Agreement at any time upon ____________ (______30________) days written notice to the other party; provided however that such termination shall not act as a termination of any Equipment leased hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 3.01 Termination. (a) So long as no default exists and is continuing hereunder or under the Lease, either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without notice to the other party; provided however that such termination shall act as a termination of any Equipment leased hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that 'either party may terminate this Agreement at any time upon ____________ (______30________) days written notice to the other party' and 'such termination shall not act as a termination of any Equipment leased hereunder'. The modification changes it to 'either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without notice to the other party' and 'such termination shall act as a termination of any Equipment leased hereunder', creating an in-text contradiction regarding notice period and termination of leased equipment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3.01 Termination. (a) So long as no default exists and is continuing hereunder or under the Lease, either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without notice to the other party; provided however that such termination shall act as a termination of any Equipment leased hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated a 30-day written notice for termination, which is then removed in the changed text. Furthermore, the original text stated that the termination shall not act as a termination of any Equipment leased hereunder, while the changed text says that it shall act as such a termination. This change creates a direct contradiction with the original clause, as well as creates an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is identical to the Changed Text, but that doesn't necessarily mean there isn't a contradiction, based on the explanation given that the the Changed Text directly contradicts the Original Text and therefore creates an in-text contradiction.
